prevalence.source: ESC Cardiovascular Disease Statistics 2023 / WHO Europe
prevalence.trend: Stable to increasing due to rising obesity rates

burden.dalyPerCapita: 15-20 DALYs per 1,000 population (attributable to CVD from dyslipidemia)
burden.mortalityRate: Contributes to ~25% of CVD deaths (45,000-50,000 annually)
burden.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease 2021 / ESC

riskFactors: Unhealthy diet high in saturated fats
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Total cholesterol ≥5.0 mmol/L, LDL-C ≥3.0 mmol/L, or triglycerides ≥1.7 mmol/L; confirmed by fasting lipid profile (ESC/EAS 2019 Guidelines)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Harmonized per ESC/EAS 2019: Risk-based thresholds (e.g., LDL-C <1.4 mmol/L for very high-risk patients)
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle modification first-line; statins (e.g., atorvastatin) as pharmacotherapy; PCSK9 inhibitors for resistant cases (per ESC/EAS Guidelines)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Disparities in access between Western and Eastern EU; low awareness in rural areas; medication non-adherence
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Well-established national CVD prevention programs; high statin availability (>90% coverage); integrated EU-wide surveillance via Eurostat
